description: Wire QuantTube channel page and upload page to real API calls, removing all hardcoded mock data. The channel page currently uses MOCK_CHANNEL, MOCK_VIDEOS, MOCK_PLAYLISTS constants instead of fetching from the API. The upload page uses simulateUpload with setTimeout instead of real upload endpoints.
steps: In apps/quantube/src/pages/channel/[id].tsx: Replace MOCK_CHANNEL, MOCK_VIDEOS, MOCK_PLAYLISTS, and the simulated setTimeout with real API calls using the apiClient pattern from apps/quantube/src/services/api-client.ts. Use useQuery from @tanstack/react-query to fetch channel data from /api/channels/{id}. Keep the mock data arrays as fallback values (use them only when the fetch fails). Add proper loading/error states using LoadingState, ErrorState from @quant/shared-ui (already imported in index.tsx).

In apps/quantube/src/pages/upload.tsx: Replace the simulateUpload function (which uses setInterval/setTimeout to fake progress) with a real upload flow. Use the apiClient to call a POST /api/videos/upload endpoint. The upload should use XMLHttpRequest or fetch with a FormData body to track real progress. Keep the existing UI flow (steps: upload, details, thumbnails, chapters, review) intact. Replace the fake 'await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 2000))' in handlePublish with a real API call to POST /api/videos (passing metadata). Add error handling and loading states.

Ensure both pages import from the apiClient service and use the existing pattern (response.success check, error handling). Keep existing mock arrays as const FALLBACK_CHANNEL etc and only use them in catch blocks.
acceptance_criteria: No MOCK_ constant is used as the primary data source in channel/[id].tsx - all data comes from API calls
No setTimeout/setInterval is used to fake upload progress in upload.tsx
Both pages have loading states shown while data is being fetched
Both pages have error states with retry capability when API calls fail
pnpm turbo typecheck passes for apps/quantube
findings: Pre-existing TS2307 error for next/router module in watch/[id].tsx also applies to channel/[id].tsx since both use useRouter from next/router. The next package types are not installed in the quantube workspace. Overall error count reduced from 78 to 74 by cleaning up unused variables/types from the original mock-based code. Prettier reformatted files during commit hook.
